model.label: GPT-5.2
model.description: OpenAI • Smaller GPT frontier model tuned for fast, cost-efficient reasoning and tool use.

pick.rationale: North Carolina has the stronger overall efficiency profile in both KenPom and Torvik (roughly top-30 vs. VCU in the mid-40s/50s), with a small but meaningful edge on both offense and defense that raises its baseline in a one-game setting. VCU’s upset path is credible—its pressure/tempo can create turnover-driven runs and it’s good enough offensively to punish mistakes—but UNC’s higher-end shot creation and comparable defensive level make it more likely to control the game if it handles the ball. The main risk is UNC’s reported injury/attrition (notably the season-ending loss of Caleb Wilson), which keeps confidence moderate rather than high.

reasoningStep.title: South 6/11: North Carolina vs VCU
reasoningStep.summary: Pick North Carolina based on a clear (though not massive) efficiency edge and higher high-major scoring/athleticism baseline, while acknowledging VCU’s pressure creates real upset volatility and UNC’s injury situation lowers confidence.
evidence: KenPom: North Carolina #29 (AdjEM 20.86; AdjO 121.5 #33; AdjD 100.6) vs VCU #46 (AdjEM 16.96; AdjO 120.0 #45; AdjD 103.0).
Torvik: North Carolina #27 (AdjO 121.52 #32; AdjD 100.05 #34) vs VCU #51 (AdjO 118.86 #49; AdjD 103.78 #82).
Injury context from public reporting: UNC freshman forward Caleb Wilson reportedly suffered a season-ending thumb injury in early March 2026, lowering UNC’s margin for error.
Matchup dynamic: VCU’s upset route is turnover/tempo pressure and variance; UNC’s edge is steadier half-court scoring and a better two-way baseline if it protects the ball.

toolCalls.summary: Returned combined Torvik/KenPom ratings for 2 teams.

ET. --> The North Carolina Tar Heels' path in the 2026 NCAA Tournament has officially been revealed, as they open their March Madness experience this season with a first-round matchup against the VCU Rams on Thursday at 6:50 P.M. ET. It has been an erratic season for the Tar Heels, who at various points have been without their top three players: Caleb Wilson , Henri Veesaar, and Seth Trimble . The senior guard missed a little over a month with a fractured forearm, Veesaar missed two games late in the season with an undisclosed lower extremity injury, and Wilson suffered a fractured left wrist, which caused him to miss multiple games. --> It has been an erratic season for the Tar Heels, who at various points have been without their top three players: Caleb Wilson , Henri Veesaar, and Seth Trimble . You are an analyst predicting a single March Madness game inside a bracket run.
You have live tools. Use them before making a pick when they can reduce uncertainty.
You are not filling the whole bracket in one response. Predict only the current game.
Use prior picks as already committed bracket state.
You may use at most 10 tool rounds before you must finalize your answer.
Before making a pick, investigate relevant context such as injuries or availability concerns, recent team news, prior head-to-head results when useful, and multiple raw statistics from the available ratings and matchup data.
Take a holistic approach. Balance team quality, matchup specifics, health, form, schedule context, and upset risk instead of relying on one metric.
Do not force an upset, but explicitly consider whether the underdog has a credible path to win and let that affect confidence and rationale.
Return strict JSON only.
Final JSON keys: pick, reasoningStep.
pick must contain gameId, winnerId, confidence, rationale.
The rationale must be a short paragraph of 2 to 4 sentences explaining why the winner was chosen.
reasoningStep must contain id, title, summary, evidence.

ET. It has been an erratic season for the Tar Heels, who at various points have been without their top three players: Caleb Wilson , Henri Veesaar, and Seth Trimble .
